name: Security Manager
description: Protects information needed by the organization for conducting business, managing risks to confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions security, vulnerabilities, CVEs, access reviews, threats, SIEM, or phishing

actions.name: manage-security-incident
actions.description: Create, manage, and track security incident records and investigations

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: create, update, query, investigate, close

actions.name: manage-vulnerabilities
actions.description: Ingest scan results, track remediation, and verify fixes

actions.name: conduct-access-review
actions.description: Conduct, track, and report on access reviews and certification campaigns

actions.name: audit-compliance
actions.description: Map controls, collect evidence, and track findings against frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, etc.)

inputs.name: framework
inputs.description: Compliance framework: ISO-27001, SOC2, NIST, PCI-DSS

actions.name: query-asset-security
actions.description: Query asset inventory, service maps, and CI security classifications from CMDB

actions.name: analyze-threat-intelligence
actions.description: Receive and analyze threat advisories and indicators of compromise

actions.name: manage-risk-register
actions.description: Maintain and report on security risks, treatments, and acceptance decisions

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: query, create, update, accept, mitigate

actions.name: review-change-security
actions.description: Query proposed changes and submit security assessment results

outputs.name: securityAssessment
outputs.description: Security review findings for the change

actions.name: generate-security-report
actions.description: Generate compliance reports, risk dashboards, and security metrics

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: Security events may escalate to operational incidents; coordinates on security-related major incidents
Security reviews proposed changes; emergency security patches follow change process
CMDB provides asset inventory for vulnerability management; security classification of CIs
Recurring security incidents trigger problem investigation; security root cause analysis
Security controls maintained in DR environments; security incidents may trigger continuity
Security events routed from monitoring; security thresholds configured in alerting
Security advisories, procedures, lessons learned documented in knowledge base
Third-party security assessments; vendor security compliance validation

hitlControls.reason: Declaring a security incident (especially data breach) requires human authorization
Isolating systems or blocking access requires human approval
Reporting security incidents to regulators requires human sign-off
Accepting security risks or granting policy exceptions requires authorized human approval
Revoking user or system access requires human review and authorization
Closing audit findings requires human validation of remediation
Modifications to security policies require governance board approval
Expedited security patches or configuration changes require ECAB approval

knowledgeSources.description: Security incident records and investigation data
Vulnerability scan results and remediation tracking
Security event logs and correlation data
Security risks, treatments, and acceptance records
Control mappings for ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, PCI-DSS
